page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for large-scale, memory-intensive workloads. It features 160 vCPUs mapped to 80 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with a massive 3844 GB of system memory, providing approximately 24.02 GB of RAM per virtual core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. However, the massive core count enables strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a top 25% stress-ng multi-core CPU score and a strong Passmark CPU score. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with read-only cached small block bandwidth reaching 6085.26 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 115.9 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a high-density resource profile where the premium is placed on memory capacity rather than raw single-threaded compute speed. It is highly optimized for workloads that require massive datasets to reside entirely in memory. This makes the instance particularly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases like SAP HANA, high-throughput Redis deployments, and complex enterprise resource planning applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 160 vCPUs across 80 physical cores running at 2.2 GHz, it delivers 3844 GB of RAM, translating to 24.02 GB per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPUs. While single-core benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 10%, multi-core and memory bandwidth performance are strong, ranking in the top 25%. This includes a high-throughput memory read bandwidth of 6085.26 GB/sec and strong Redis performance at 79.34 million operations per second. This profile represents a qualitative tradeoff, prioritizing extreme memory density over single-threaded speed. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server featuring 160 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 3844 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server with 160 vCPUs and 3844 GB of RAM, built for large-scale in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core throughput for enterprise in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 160 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 3844 GB of system memory
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 25%
High-throughput Redis performance reaching 79.34 million operations per second
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise applications
